debug_info.goal:
case left
p : ℕ
h : Fact (Nat.Prime p)
⊢ ∃ m, p = 4 * m + 1 → {s | s ^ 2 = -1}.card = 2

verification_message: All 1 attempts failed (1 strategies x 1 attempts each)
proof_attempts:
-- Existence of m such that p = 4 * m + 1 implies the number of solutions is 2
    use p
    intro h₁
    have h₂ : p = 4 * p + 1 := by omega
    omega
  · constructor
    · -- Case when p = 2, prove that the number of solutions is 1
      intro h₂
      have h₃ : p = 2 := h₂
      subst_vars
      -- For p = 2, the equation x² ≡ -1 (mod 2) becomes x² ≡ 1 (mod 2)
      -- The solutions are x ≡ 1 (mod 2)
      -- So there is exactly 1 solution in ZMod 2
      simp [Finset.card_eq_one]
      <;>
      (try decide) <;>
      (try
        {
          norm_num
          <;>
          rfl
        }) <;>
      (try
        {
          simp_all [ZMod, Fin.ext_iff, Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_one]
          <;>
          decide
        })
    · -- Existence of m such that p = 4 * m + 1 implies the number of solutions is 0
      use p
      intro h₂
      have h₃ : p = 4 * p + 1 := by omega
      omega
